item.message: Exceeded skills context budget of 2%. All skill descriptions were removed and 7 additional skills were not included in the model-visible skills list.

item.text: I’ll keep this self-contained in the candidate directory and avoid the ThreeNative tooling entirely. I’m going to inspect the existing files first, then add the smallest playable canvas game with local assets/code.

The directory only has benchmark metadata, so there is no existing app shape to preserve. I’m going to add `index.html`, `styles.css`, and `game.js` with a canvas-only implementation: a movable launcher, limited shots, target knockdown scoring, and restart controls.

The first pass is in place. I noticed one state bug while reviewing it: target “home” positions need to be reset every round, otherwise displacement scoring can break after retry, so I’m tightening that before running it.
Now I’m going to serve the static page locally and exercise it in a browser context so I can confirm the canvas renders and the keyboard path changes game state.

item.text: The page loads; the only console error is a missing favicon request, not game code. I’m adding a no-op favicon link so the benchmark run stays clean, then I’ll run interaction checks.

item.text: The reload is clean now. I’m checking two things next: rendered canvas pixels so it is visibly drawing, and a keyboard-driven shot path that can knock targets and update the score.

content.text:
### Result
{
  "title": "Knockdown Launcher",
  "canvasCssSize": [
    780,
    520
  ],
  "canvasPixels": [
    780,
    520
  ],
  "sampledColoredPixels": 4056,
  "score": "0",
  "shots": "8",
  "targets": "0/12",
  "message": "Move with A/D or arrow keys. Aim with W/S. Hold Space, release to launch."
}
### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => {\n  const canvas = document.querySelector(\'#game\');\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const ctx = canvas.getContext(\'2d\');\n  const data = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height).data;\n  let colored = 0;\n  for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i += 400) {\n    if (data[i] || data[i + 1] || data[i + 2]) colored += 1;\n  }\n  return {\n    title: document.title,\n    canvasCssSize: [Math.round(rect.width), Math.round(rect.height)],\n    canvasPixels: [canvas.width, canvas.height],\n    sampledColoredPixels: colored,\n    score: document.querySelector(\'#score\').textContent,\n    shots: document.querySelector(\'#shots\').textContent,\n    targets: document.querySelector(\'#targets\').textContent,\n    message: document.querySelector(\'#message\').textContent\n  };\n}');
```

### Ran Playwright code
```js
// Press Enter
await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
```
### Page
- Page URL: http://127.0.0.1:4173/index.html
- Page Title: Knockdown Launcher
### Snapshot
- [Snapshot](.playwright-mcp/page-2026-07-07T04-59-30-585Z.yml)

### Result
{
  "score": "0",
  "shots": "8",
  "targets": "0/12",
  "message": "Move with A/D or arrow keys. Aim with W/S. Hold Space, release to launch."
}
### Ran Playwright code
```js
await page.evaluate('() => ({ score: document.querySelector(\'#score\').textContent, shots: document.querySelector(\'#shots\').textContent, targets: document.querySelector(\'#targets\').textContent, message: document.querySelector(\'#message\').textContent })');
```

item.text: The browser check passed: the canvas rendered nonblank, a keyboard-driven move-and-launch knocked one target down, score advanced to 100, shots decremented, and Enter reset the round. I’m stopping the local server and doing a quick file/status check.

item.text: The app files are self-contained and contain no ThreeNative/tn/Bevy references. I’m removing the transient Playwright logs and screenshot I created during verification so the candidate directory stays focused on the deliverable.

Playable browser game is complete in this directory:

- `index.html`
- `styles.css`
- `game.js`

It uses plain browser canvas JavaScript, no ThreeNative/tn/Bevy APIs. Controls: A/D or arrow keys move/aim, Space charges and launches, Enter/R retries.

Verified by serving with `python3 -m http.server 4173` and using Playwright to confirm the canvas rendered, keyboard input moved/launched, a target was knocked down, score updated to `100`, shots decremented, and Enter reset the round.
